title: Laboratory renovation successfully completed
content:
While the upgraded facilities are now operational, some adjustments and installations are still ongoing. We kindly ask for your patience and understanding during this transition period as we finalize all arrangements.

The renovation has significantly improved our laboratory capabilities with the addition of modern equipment and facilities, including:

  • Two new fume hoods 
  • New drying ovens, increasing the total number available to eight
  • A high-quality ultrapure water purification system (Milli-Q)
  • A laboratory dishwasher capable of ultrapure water rinsing and acid washing
  • A new dual-compressor ultra-low temperature freezer for reliable sample storage

    • Access to the laboratory wing is secured via a door code, which can be obtained from the research coordinator.

    Please note that certain instruments and laboratory functions require prior training before use.

Veli Lehtovaara, our artist in residency, choreographer and  performer, will guide a sensory focused somatic session.

title: Yaniv Brandvain visiting
content:
Since April 2026, we have had a pleasure to host Professor Yaniv Brandvain from the University of Minnesota as a visiting scholar. We have had a blast combining theoretical models with our fresh pine embryogeny and gene expression data.

title: Robert Kesälahti defended his thesis
content:
On May 22nd, Robert Kesälahti defended his PhD thesis "Optimizing genomic data generation methods and uncovering parent-of-origin effects in Pinus sylvestris". We had a pleasure to have Nathaniel Street from Umeå University to act as Robert's opponent. Congratulations, Robert!

title: Uusia akatemiahankkeita ja akatemiatutkijoita Helsingin yliopistoon
content:
Researchers from the University of Helsinki performed well in the Research Council of Finland’s winter call. 44 members of the university received Academy Research Fellowship funding, and 79 researchers were awarded Academy Project funding. The total funding awarded to the University of Helsinki amounts to €71.8 million.

The funding period in the Academy Research Fellowship and Academy Project funding schemes is four years.

Academy Research Fellowship funding is intended for early-career researchers on a fast career track who have formed international networks and who are conducting scientifically high-quality and high-impact research that contributes to scientific renewal.

The aim of the Academy Project Funding is to promote the regeneration and broad range of Finnish science and to improve the quality and scientific and other impact of research. The aim is to attain internationally as high a scientific standard of work as possible and to support scientific breakthroughs and top-tier international research collaboration. 

Below, you will find all new Academy Research Fellows and Academy Projects at the University of Helsinki.

creator: katitoiv@helsinki.fi

title: New materials enable cleaner rare earth elements separation
content:
Rare earth elements (REEs) are essential for everyday technologies such as smartphones, LED lights, wind turbines and many medical applications. At the same time, supply chains are under pressure due to the geographical concentration of production, and electronic waste is growing worldwide.

One of the challenges with mining REEs is that they occur only in small amounts and behave almost identically in chemical terms. This makes separating them individually a massive technical challenge.

Doctoral researcher  at the University of Helsinki has developed a series of new hybrid materials that can selectively separate REEs from one another. The materials combine an organic component (aminophosphonates) with an inorganic zirconium-oxide framework. The developed solid hybrid materials can selectively bind and distinguish between chemically similar REEs without relying on harsh or hazardous chemicals during the initial separation stages, offering a cleaner and safer alternative to traditional methods.

creator: merimaaj@helsinki.fi

title: Metsästys ja silakan koko muokkaavat olennaisesti harmaahylkeen tulevaisuutta Itämerellä
content:
The Baltic grey seal population took a historic dive in the 1970s to just some 3,000 individuals following excessive hunting and proliferation of organochlorines (such as DDT and PCB) used in pesticides and paint. At the same time, the population size of the Baltic Sea’s other seal species, the Baltic ringed seal, dropped dramatically to 5,000 individuals. 

After these setbacks, seal hunting was banned in the Baltic Sea in the 1980s and the use of substances harmful to seals was regulated. Thanks to these measures, seal populations began to grow.

A study led by the University of Helsinki examines developments in Baltic grey seal populations. Since the 1970s, the Baltic grey seal population has grown to 57,000 individuals. A University of Helsinki –led study revealed the impact of herring weight on seal population growth. As the average weight of herring continues to increase, the number of seal pups could grow by as much as 10% every year.

title: Puutarhan parhaat palat – Kesän maksuttomat opastukset Kaisaniemessä
content:
On a guided tour, you’ll journey through millions of years of evolution along the paths of the Evolution Garden, and immerse yourself in a world of scents and colours in the Garden of the senses. You’ll also admire unique lichen and moss gardens, while hearing stories about the garden’s past and present, and the buildings found in the area.

Tours will take place from June 23 to August 22, 2026. Free entrance, no prior registration required.

  • Tuesdays, Fridays and Saturdays at 15 (3 pm)

    • The tour starts at the greenhouse entrance and lasts approximately 40 minutes.

    Kaisaniemenranta 2, Helsinki

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

A temporary bridge for pedestrians will be built on the east side of Arkadiankatu over the Baana. The aim is to maintain the pedestrian route on the east side of the bridge until the temporary bridge is operational. Cyclists will also be able to use the bridge.

The Baana route underneath the bridge is expected to remain open for most of the renovation's duration, with some minor exceptions.

There will be changes to the motor vehicle routes by the bridge and around it in January. If possible, should be favoured during the bridge renovation.

Connections to the bus terminals in Kamppi will remain available, but there will be a slight diversion of routes.

Trams 1 and 1T will run via Simonkatu and Kamppi. Lasipalatsi and the Natural History Museum will not be served.

The diversion is due to the renovation of the Arkadiankatu bridge and will remain in place until late 2026. The renovation will not affect HSL's bus services. 

Pohjoinen Rautatiekatu 13

title: Ukrainan sota - yliopiston suositukset
content:
The University of Helsinki has condemned the military action by Russia and expressed its support for Ukraine and its university community. We have also expressed our support for Ukraine, together with other Finnish universities, through statements by and the . As a demonstration of support, we have illuminated buildings in the colours of the Ukrainian flag and raised the flag on our premises.

One of the principles of the University of Helsinki is to promote international collaboration in research and higher education, and to support these efforts even in difficult circumstances. Russia’s indefensible military aggression in Ukraine has required exceptional measures, which include those taken in the collaboration between universities.

The University of Helsinki is monitoring both national and EU-level sanctions, adapting our policies and processes accordingly. Together with other Finnish and international universities, the University of Helsinki strives to promote the end of military action in Ukraine, while supporting Ukrainian institutions and people.

creator: terojuut@helsinki.fi

title: Suomalaiset odottavat päättäjiltä purkupalloa kansainvälisen järjestelmän jännitteisiin
content:
Citizens’ attitudes towards the United States are significantly critical, while Finland’s official line emphasises close bilateral relations and multifaceted cooperation. New divisions are emerging in foreign and security policy, which is becoming one of the key issues in the 2027 parliamentary elections.

These results were published in a new research report, “” . The report is part of the Dynamic Support for Security and Defence Policy (NATOpoll) research project led by the University of Helsinki, in which the same respondents are surveyed at regular intervals.

The international security environment is undergoing an exceptionally intense and multi-layered transformation. The rules-based international system has lost much of its steering power, while a new order has yet to emerge. Global politics is now characterised by an in-between state where major powers are increasingly openly pursuing complex geo-economic interests.
snippet: Citizens’ attitudes towards the United States are significantly critical, while Finland’s official line emphasises close bilateral relations and multifaceted cooperation. New divisions are emerging in
creator: pakomula@helsinki.fi
